texto: Do you realize that you will not only wreck your civilization, such as it is, and kill most of your people; but that you will also poison the fish in your rivers, the squirrels in your trees, the flocks of birds, the soil, the water? There are times when you seem, to us, like apes loose in a museum, carrying knives, slashing the canvases, breaking the statuary with hammers.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Walter Tevis: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “Do you realize that you will not only wreck your civilization, such as it is, and kill most of yo…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, humanity, realize pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.

There are times when you seem, to us, like apes loose in a museum, carrying knives, slashing the canvases, breaking the statuary with hammers.

texto: I feel free and strong. If I were not a reader of books I could not feel this way. Whatever may happen to me, thank God that I can read, that I have truly touched the minds of other men.
explicacao: Citação de Walter Tevis sobre temas ligados a gratidao. “I feel free and strong. If I were not a reader of books I could not feel this way. Whatever may h…” articula gratidao, literacy, reading em registro de gratidao, com relevância para contextos como reading.
